species: N2O-respiring bacterial enrichment culture, dominant growing population affiliated with MAG260 (closest cultured relative: Azonexus sp., isolate AN, 98.2% ANI to MAG260)

growth_records.strain: MAG260 / isolate AN (Azonexus sp.), mixed enrichment (not axenic culture)
growth_records.medium: Anaerobic biogas digestate (municipal wastewater sludge, poly-aluminum-chloride/ferric-chloride precipitated) amended with N2O in a He headspace
growth_records.cite: Jonassen et al. Nitrous oxide respiring bacteria in biogas digestates for reduced agricultural emissions. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01101-x. [PMC8776835]
growth_records.snippet: The modeling indicated that the cell density of the growing NRB increased exponentially (specific growth rate, µ = 0.1 h-1) from a very low initial density (~2.5x10^3 cells mL-1) to 1.6x10^8 cells mL-1 after 110 h.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate mu=0.1 h-1 was derived by the authors from a kinetic model of N2 production/N2O reduction fit to represent two subpopulations (one exponentially growing), and cross-validated here: ln(1.6e8/2.5e3 cells/mL)/110 h = ln(64000)/110 = 11.07/110 = 0.101 h-1, consistent with the reported 0.1 h-1 (calculation from numbers explicitly in the paper: initial density ~2.5e3 cells/mL, density 1.6e8 cells/mL at t=110h). This is a COMMUNITY-level (mixed enrichment) modeled growth rate, not a pure-culture measurement. Taxonomic attribution to MAG260/Azonexus sp. is inferential: MAG260 was one of two MAGs identified as 'growing' by metagenome/metaproteome coverage regression and accounted for 92% of the total detectable Nos (N2O reductase) protein pool at the final timepoint, and isolate AN (Azonexus sp.) shared 98.2% ANI with MAG260, but the mu=0.1 h-1 value itself was derived from bulk N2O/N2 gas kinetics of the whole enrichment, not an isolate-specific measurement. Confidence marked low due to lack of species-pure-culture resolution. Enrichment incubation temperature (20C) explicitly stated in text; incubated at 20C rather than digester temperatures (37/52C) to avoid selecting for organisms outside the normal soil temperature range.
